destinations.tagline: Joshua Tree National Park has become a top destination for Black outdoor enthusiasts seeking dese...
destinations.vibe: Joshua Tree National Park has become a top destination for Black outdoor enthusiasts seeking desert landscapes, rock climbing, stargazing, and Instagram-worthy shots among the iconic trees.
destinations.safetyNotes: Joshua Tree is safe and welcoming. The national park and surrounding towns are tourist-friendly. Desert survival basics apply — water is essential, heat can be extreme.

neighborhoods.name: Joshua Tree (town)
neighborhoods.description: Small desert town with galleries, yoga studios, and Airbnbs

neighborhoods.name: Twentynine Palms
neighborhoods.description: Gateway to the eastern park entrance, Marine Corps base nearby

neighborhoods.name: Palm Springs (nearby)
neighborhoods.description: Desert resort city 45 mins from the park — Black-owned resorts emerging

mustVisit.name: Joshua Tree National Park
mustVisit.description: Where the Mojave meets the Colorado Desert — surreal landscapes and unique Joshua trees

mustVisit.name: Cholla Cactus Garden
mustVisit.description: 0.25-mile loop through stunning teddy bear cholla cactus garden

mustVisit.name: Keys View Overlook
mustVisit.description: Panoramic views of Coachella Valley, Salton Sea, and San Jacinto Mountains

mustVisit.name: Skull Rock Nature Trail
mustVisit.description: Easy 1.7-mile trail through fascinating rock formations

mustVisit.name: Stargazing at the park
mustVisit.description: Among the best stargazing in Southern California — International Dark Sky Park

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens
destinations.ratingNotes: Desert park; growing Black outdoors scene; limited BOP

destinations.tagline: A city of empires, ferries, and sensory overload.
destinations.vibe: Istanbul feels cinematic and massive, with layers of history, food, religion, and nightlife all colliding at once. For Black travelers, it can be exciting and memorable, though the city rewards preparation because the experience shifts dramatically by neighborhood and current political climate.
destinations.safetyNotes: Most visits center on busy tourist districts that are manageable, but demonstrations, scams, traffic, and regional political risk lower the overall comfort score. Dress and move with local awareness, use licensed transport, and stay updated on current advisories.

neighborhoods.name: Sultanahmet
neighborhoods.description: Historic core home to the city’s headline monuments.

neighborhoods.name: Karaköy
neighborhoods.description: Waterside district mixing cafés, galleries, and ferry access.

neighborhoods.name: Kadıköy
neighborhoods.description: Asian-side favorite known for food, nightlife, and local energy.

mustVisit.name: Hagia Sophia
mustVisit.description: World-famous monument reflecting the city’s layered past.

mustVisit.name: Grand Bazaar
mustVisit.description: Historic market experience full of color and commerce.

mustVisit.name: Topkapı Palace
mustVisit.description: Former Ottoman palace with major historical collections.

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for U.S. citizen for short tourist stays in Türkiye.
destinations.ratingNotes: Eurasian crossroads; some discrimination; limited BOP

destinations.tagline: Central Valley practicality with park-gateway appeal
destinations.vibe: Fresno is less about polished city tourism and more about access, affordability, and the feeling of being in the middle of California's agricultural engine. For a Black traveler, it works best as a grounded base for food, family-style hospitality, and nearby national park adventures rather than a nightlife-first destination.
destinations.safetyNotes: Fresno requires a bit more planning than some other California stops, especially at night and across spread-out districts. Focus on the Tower District, Woodward Park side, and well-reviewed venues, and treat it as a drive-based city rather than one to freestyle on foot after dark.

neighborhoods.name: Tower District
neighborhoods.description: The city's best-known arts and nightlife corridor with theaters and bars.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown Fresno
neighborhoods.description: Historic core with civic buildings, murals, and event venues.

neighborhoods.name: Woodward Park Area
neighborhoods.description: Cleaner, calmer side of town with parks and easier lodging options.

mustVisit.name: Forestiere Underground Gardens
mustVisit.description: Unusual historic attraction that gives Fresno a distinctive signature stop.

mustVisit.name: Fresno Art Museum
mustVisit.description: Compact museum adding cultural depth to a Central Valley stay.

mustVisit.name: Tower District
mustVisit.description: Most reliable area for dining, drinks, and evening activity.

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizen
destinations.ratingNotes: Central Valley; moderate Black community; limited BOP

destinations.tagline: Andes-framed capital with big-city rhythm and wine-country reach
destinations.vibe: Santiago feels modern, spread out, and very livable, with a polished business-city shell around distinct neighborhood scenes. From a Black traveler perspective, it can feel efficient more than warm at first, but the food, views, and day-trip potential make it rewarding.
destinations.safetyNotes: Use city awareness around crowded transit, downtown protests, and nightlife zones late at night. Black travelers may encounter curiosity or occasional stereotyping, but the bigger practical issue is the usual urban mix of pickpocketing and choosing the right base neighborhood.

neighborhoods.name: Lastarria
neighborhoods.description: Central cultural quarter with museums, dining, and boutique stays.

neighborhoods.name: Bellavista
neighborhoods.description: Colorful nightlife and restaurant district near Cerro San Cristóbal.

neighborhoods.name: Providencia
neighborhoods.description: Comfortable residential-commercial district popular with visitors.

mustVisit.name: Cerro San Cristóbal
mustVisit.description: Hilltop park and lookout with some of the city’s best panoramic views.

mustVisit.name: La Moneda / Centro Histórico
mustVisit.description: Historic and political heart of Santiago with key landmarks nearby.

mustVisit.name: Museo Chileno de Arte Precolombino
mustVisit.description: Top museum for Indigenous and pre-Columbian art across the Americas.

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for U.S. citizen for short tourist stays
destinations.ratingNotes: Chile; limited Afro-Chilean community; some discrimination

destinations.tagline: Zimbabwe’s second city with heritage, museums, and a more grounded pace.
destinations.vibe: Bulawayo feels calmer and more heritage-driven than Harare, making it a strong choice if you want history without nonstop capital-city motion. For Black travelers, it often reads as lower-stress, especially when used as a base for cultural and nature side trips.
destinations.safetyNotes: Generally calmer than larger capitals, but service consistency, cash logistics, and basic urban precautions still matter. Keep transport arranged after dark and do not assume quieter streets mean zero risk.

neighborhoods.name: CBD
neighborhoods.description: Historic central area with museums, hotels, and older architecture.

neighborhoods.name: Hillside
neighborhoods.description: Residential district with a calmer feel and practical lodging options.

neighborhoods.name: Matsheumhlope
neighborhoods.description: Area near the university and greener stretches of the city.

mustVisit.name: Natural History Museum of Zimbabwe
mustVisit.description: Major museum with strong collections on science and heritage.

mustVisit.name: Nesbitt Castle
mustVisit.description: Distinctive landmark property known for architecture and events.

mustVisit.name: Matobo National Park
mustVisit.description: Important nearby landscape for rock art, scenery, and wildlife.

destinations.visaInfo: Visa may be required or issued on arrival for U.S. citizen visiting Zimbabwe; verify current entry rules before travel.
destinations.ratingNotes: Zimbabwe second city; Black-majority; heritage focus

destinations.tagline: Waterfront grit with artsy Pacific Northwest soul
destinations.vibe: Tacoma feels like a more grounded, creative alternative to Seattle, with museums, waterfront views, and a strong independent streak. From a Black traveler’s perspective, it works best as a low-key culture-and-food trip where you build around museums, local coffee, and scenic neighborhoods rather than nonstop nightlife.
destinations.safetyNotes: Tacoma is workable for visitors, especially around Ruston Way, Proctor, and the museum district in daytime. Stay alert in quieter stretches downtown late at night, use rideshare after drinks, and keep normal city awareness around parking lots and transit hubs.

neighborhoods.name: Museum District
neighborhoods.description: Downtown cultural core with museums, theaters, and easy walking access.

neighborhoods.name: Proctor
neighborhoods.description: Beloved local district with cafés, boutiques, and a neighborhood feel.

neighborhoods.name: Ruston Way Waterfront
neighborhoods.description: Scenic waterfront strip for views, dining, and sunset walks.

mustVisit.name: Museum of Glass
mustVisit.description: Tacoma signature museum celebrating glass art and bold design.

mustVisit.name: Point Defiance Park
mustVisit.description: Huge urban park with trails, gardens, and Puget Sound views.

mustVisit.name: LeMay - America’s Car Museum
mustVisit.description: Striking museum experience for car lovers and design fans alike.

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizen
destinations.ratingNotes: Pacific Northwest; Black community present; waterfront city

destinations.tagline: Beach city energy with nightlife and startup polish.
destinations.vibe: Tel Aviv usually balances beach ease, food, and nightlife better than most cities in the region. For Black travelers, it can feel open and modern in daily social spaces, but the broader security environment matters more here than in almost any other Batch 6 destination.
destinations.safetyNotes: Under normal conditions Tel Aviv can be easy to enjoy, but current regional security risks change the equation significantly. Anyone considering travel should monitor official advisories closely, stay flexible, and avoid treating the city like a standard carefree beach trip.

neighborhoods.name: Neve Tzedek
neighborhoods.description: Charming historic district with boutiques and café culture.

neighborhoods.name: Florentin
neighborhoods.description: Creative neighborhood known for street art and nightlife.

neighborhoods.name: Jaffa
neighborhoods.description: Ancient port area blending history, food, and sea views.

mustVisit.name: Tel Aviv Beachfront
mustVisit.description: Long urban coastline central to the city’s lifestyle.

mustVisit.name: Carmel Market
mustVisit.description: Bustling market for food, snacks, and everyday city life.

mustVisit.name: Tel Aviv Museum of Art
mustVisit.description: Major museum anchoring the city’s cultural scene.

destinations.visaInfo: U.S. citizen tourist travel is generally visa-free for short stays, subject to current entry and security conditions.
destinations.ratingNotes: Ethiopian Jewish community faces discrimination; documented racism

destinations.tagline: Kansas City's 18th & Vine district is one of America's greatest jazz and blues heritage districts...
destinations.vibe: Kansas City's 18th & Vine district is one of America's greatest jazz and blues heritage districts. The American Jazz Museum and Negro Leagues Baseball Museum make it essential for Black history travelers.
destinations.safetyNotes: Kansas City has some elevated crime in certain areas. The 18th & Vine district and nearby Crown Center are safe for visitors. The Country Club Plaza and Crossroads Arts District are very accessible.

neighborhoods.name: 18th & Vine Historic Jazz District
neighborhoods.description: Charlie Parker, Count Basie, and Jay McShann's birthplace — heart of KC jazz history

neighborhoods.name: Crossroads Arts District
neighborhoods.description: KC's creative hub with First Friday galleries and diverse community

neighborhoods.name: Westport
neighborhoods.description: KC's entertainment district with bars and live music

mustVisit.name: Negro Leagues Baseball Museum
mustVisit.description: The most comprehensive museum of Black baseball history — KC was home to the Monarchs, Satchel Paige's team

mustVisit.name: American Jazz Museum
mustVisit.description: Celebrates Kansas City's towering contribution to jazz — Charlie Parker birthplace

mustVisit.name: Kansas City BBQ (Joe's, Gates, Jack Stack)
mustVisit.description: KC BBQ is a world religion — Gates Bar-B-Q is Black-owned and legendary since 1946

mustVisit.name: Gem Theater (18th & Vine)
mustVisit.description: Restored historic jazz club at 18th & Vine — live performances continue

mustVisit.name: Westport Civil War history
mustVisit.description: Battle of Westport 1864 — significant for Black USCT soldiers

destinations.bestTimeToVisit: April-October (avoid harsh winters), Jazz & Heritage Festival (June)
destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens
destinations.ratingNotes: 18th & Vine jazz heritage; strong Black community; growing BOP

destinations.tagline: Southernmost point of the continental US with a colorful, diverse, and accepting culture. Popular...
destinations.vibe: Southernmost point of the continental US with a colorful, diverse, and accepting culture. Popular Black-friendly destination especially among LGBTQ+ Black travelers. Fort Zachary Taylor and vibrant Duval Street culture.
destinations.safetyNotes: Key West is one of the safest and most welcoming places for all travelers, including Black LGBTQ+ travelers. Very inclusive and progressive. Old Town is completely walkable.

neighborhoods.name: Old Town / Duval Street
neighborhoods.description: Key West's famous entertainment and dining strip

neighborhoods.name: Bahama Village
neighborhoods.description: Historic Bahamian and Cuban immigrant community — the Black and Caribbean heart of Key West

neighborhoods.name: Midtown / New Town
neighborhoods.description: Where Key West residents actually live

mustVisit.name: Bahama Village Historic District
mustVisit.description: Built by Bahamian immigrants — Petronia Street has the best local restaurants and culture

mustVisit.name: Southernmost Point Buoy
mustVisit.description: Most photographed landmark in Florida — the line is long but worth it

mustVisit.name: Fort Zachary Taylor Historic State Park
mustVisit.description: Civil War fort with the best beach on Key West — also has USCT Black soldier history

mustVisit.name: Louie's Backyard
mustVisit.description: Iconic oceanfront restaurant — sunset dining at its finest

mustVisit.name: Hemingway Home and Museum
mustVisit.description: Ernest Hemingway's home with 60 polydactyl cats — an only-in-Key-West experience

mustVisit.name: Fantasy Fest (October)
mustVisit.description: Ten-day Halloween carnival — extremely popular with LGBTQ+ and Black travelers

destinations.bestTimeToVisit: November-April (best weather), October for Fantasy Fest
destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens
destinations.ratingNotes: Colorful; accepting; but very limited Black-owned presence

destinations.tagline: Afro-Mexican rhythm on the Gulf with a port-city pulse
destinations.vibe: Veracruz has warmth, music, and coastal energy that feels more lived-in than polished, which is part of the appeal. For Black travelers, it stands out because Afro-Mexican heritage, dance, and food feel closer to the surface here than in many better-known Mexico destinations.
destinations.safetyNotes: Veracruz can be rewarding, but it is better approached with practical caution than carefree beach-town assumptions. Stay in known visitor areas, use trusted transportation at night, and keep plans focused on the boardwalk, historic center, and organized outings rather than drifting too far without local guidance.

neighborhoods.name: Malecon / Waterfront
neighborhoods.description: Seafront stretch for breezes, snacks, music, and casual strolling.

neighborhoods.name: Centro Historico
neighborhoods.description: Historic core with plazas, colonial buildings, and port-city atmosphere.

neighborhoods.name: Boca del Rio
neighborhoods.description: Modern adjacent zone with hotels, dining, and beach access.

mustVisit.name: San Juan de Ulua
mustVisit.description: Historic fort tied to colonial history and the city’s port legacy.

mustVisit.name: Malecon de Veracruz
mustVisit.description: Classic waterfront promenade for people-watching and city energy.

mustVisit.name: Aquarium of Veracruz
mustVisit.description: Popular family-friendly attraction and easy add-on to a waterfront day.

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizen for short tourist stays

destinations.tagline: The Afrobeats capital of the world
destinations.vibe: The Afrobeats capital of the world — chaotic, electric, ambitious, and deeply alive.
destinations.safetyNotes: Lagos is a megacity of contrasts. The Island (Victoria Island, Lekki, Ikoyi) is very safe, modern, and full of incredible restaurants and nightlife. The Mainland requires more caution — avoid displaying wealth and use vetted drivers rather than street taxis. Uber and Bolt are reliable and strongly recommended. Traffic (called 'go-slow') is infamous — build extra time into every plan. Nigerians are incredibly warm to the diaspora.

neighborhoods.name: Victoria Island
neighborhoods.description: Lagos's business and luxury hub — rooftop bars, high-end restaurants, embassies, and the best hotels.

neighborhoods.name: Lekki
neighborhoods.description: Trendy, upscale neighborhood with the best Afrobeats clubs, brunch spots, art galleries, and nightlife in Lagos.

neighborhoods.name: Ikoyi
neighborhoods.description: Quiet, leafy, and affluent — beautiful colonial architecture, boutique restaurants, and a calm contrast to the city.

neighborhoods.name: Balogun Market
neighborhoods.description: One of the largest markets in West Africa — fabrics, fashion, electronics. The Mainland hustle at its finest.

mustVisit.name: Nike Art Gallery
mustVisit.description: The largest art gallery in West Africa — five floors of Nigerian art, textiles, and beadwork. Chief Nike Davies-Okundaye's life work. Do not miss it.

mustVisit.name: Afrobeats Nightlife (Lekki)
mustVisit.description: Experience Afrobeats in its birthplace. Venues like Quilox, Circa, and Club 57 are legendary. Go Thursday-Saturday.

mustVisit.name: Terra Kulture
mustVisit.description: Arts complex showcasing Nigerian culture — theater, gallery, restaurant, and bookshop. A beautiful cultural anchor.
mustVisit.address: 1376 Tiamiyu Savage St, Victoria Island, Lagos

mustVisit.name: Freedom Park
mustVisit.description: Built on a colonial-era prison, now a stunning arts and events space. Live music, food, and history in one place.

destinations.visaInfo: Visa required for most nationalities. Nigerian e-Visa available online. US citizens apply in advance.
destinations.ratingNotes: EndSARS police risk real; but community welcome and BOP extremely high

destinations.tagline: Southwest Louisiana's Mardi Gras celebration and vibrant Creole culture. Lake Charles is a hub fo...
destinations.vibe: Southwest Louisiana's Mardi Gras celebration and vibrant Creole culture. Lake Charles is a hub for Zydeco music, Cajun cuisine, and gambling — popular with Houston's large Black community for weekend trips.
destinations.safetyNotes: Lake Charles is generally safe in tourist and casino areas. The city is rebuilding after Hurricane Laura (2020). Downtown and lakefront areas are accessible.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown Lake Charles
neighborhoods.description: Louisiana casino resort town with Creole cultural roots

mustVisit.name: Lake Charles Mardi Gras
mustVisit.description: Smaller than NOLA but authentically Cajun — crawfish-themed krewe floats unique to LC

mustVisit.name: Contraband Days
mustVisit.description: Annual May festival on the waterfront — pirate themed with concerts and events

mustVisit.name: L'Auberge Casino Resort
mustVisit.description: Premier casino resort on the lakefront

mustVisit.name: Crying Eagle Brewing
mustVisit.description: Local craft brewery in a renovated factory — excellent food and beer

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens
destinations.ratingNotes: Southwest Louisiana Creole culture; Mardi Gras; limited BOP

destinations.tagline: Westside Las Vegas has a rich but largely unknown Black history. The Strip was segregated — Black...
destinations.vibe: Westside Las Vegas has a rich but largely unknown Black history. The Strip was segregated — Black performers had to stay in Westside. Today, Black travelers love Vegas for entertainment, nightlife, and multiple Black celebrity residencies.
destinations.safetyNotes: The Strip and tourist areas are generally very safe. The Westside historical area is safe to visit during the day. As everywhere, basic street awareness applies late at night. It's one of the most visited US cities overall.

neighborhoods.name: Las Vegas Westside
neighborhoods.description: Historic Black Las Vegas — where Nat King Cole, Sammy Davis Jr., and Louis Armstrong slept when barred from Strip hotels

neighborhoods.name: The Strip
neighborhoods.description: World's entertainment capital — countless Black performers headline here

neighborhoods.name: Downtown Fremont
neighborhoods.description: Older, grittier Vegas with arts scene and vintage casino culture

neighborhoods.name: Summerlin / Henderson
neighborhoods.description: Growing diverse suburban communities popular with Black families

mustVisit.name: Westside Las Vegas Historical Society Museum
mustVisit.description: Documents the history of Black Las Vegas and the iconic Moulin Rouge Hotel (first racially integrated casino)

mustVisit.name: Black celebrity residencies and concerts
mustVisit.description: Usher, Celine Dion era — check current Black artist residencies at Park MGM, Caesars

mustVisit.name: Vegas Uncork'd / Black Restaurant Week
mustVisit.description: Growing Black restaurant scene in Las Vegas

mustVisit.name: Henderson Bird Viewing Preserve
mustVisit.description: Nature escape near Las Vegas

mustVisit.name: Red Rock Canyon
mustVisit.description: Stunning red rock formations 20 minutes from the Strip — great for day hikes

mustVisit.name: National Finals Rodeo
mustVisit.description: Major event each December — significant Black cowboy history celebrated here

destinations.bestTimeToVisit: October-April (avoid brutal desert summer heat), New Year's Eve for spectacular celebrations
destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens
destinations.ratingNotes: Historic Westside Black community; entertainment access; police moderate

destinations.tagline: Kentucky State University is in Frankfort (nearby). Lexington is home to the International Museum...
destinations.vibe: Kentucky State University is in Frankfort (nearby). Lexington is home to the International Museum of the Horse and Thoroughbred Racing — Black jockeys dominated Kentucky Derby history. Muhammad Ali is from Louisville.
destinations.safetyNotes: Lexington is generally safe and is a college town (UK). The historic district and downtown are welcoming.

neighborhoods.name: Cheapside (Historic Downtown)
neighborhoods.description: Cheapside was one of the largest slave markets in the nation — essential Black history

neighborhoods.name: East End
neighborhoods.description: Lexington's historic African American neighborhood

mustVisit.name: Cheapside Slave Market Historic Site
mustVisit.description: One of America's largest slave markets — Henry Clay sold slaves here, marker in downtown Lexington

mustVisit.name: Muhammad Ali Center (Louisville, 80 min)
mustVisit.description: Louisville's magnificent tribute to The Greatest — essential Black American pilgrimage

mustVisit.name: Kentucky Horse Park
mustVisit.description: International Museum of the Horse — Black jockeys won the first 15 Kentucky Derbies

mustVisit.name: Keeneland Race Course
mustVisit.description: One of America's most beautiful thoroughbred racing venues — April and October race meets

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens
destinations.ratingNotes: Kentucky; Kentucky State nearby; Muhammad Ali connection

destinations.tagline: Africa’s diplomatic capital with coffee culture, altitude, and deep history.
destinations.vibe: Addis feels intellectual, fast-changing, and rooted in pan-African significance, especially if you care about politics, history, and culture. For Black travelers, there is something powerful about being in a city so central to continental identity, though infrastructure and security shifts can change the mood quickly.
destinations.safetyNotes: Conditions can change fast because of political and security issues, so real-time monitoring matters more here than in easier capitals. Use trusted hotels and drivers, avoid demonstrations, keep movement simple after dark, and stay current on official advisories.

neighborhoods.name: Bole
neighborhoods.description: Airport-adjacent district with hotels, restaurants, and nightlife.

neighborhoods.name: Kazanchis
neighborhoods.description: Central business area with major hotels and meeting spaces.

neighborhoods.name: Piazza
neighborhoods.description: Historic area with old architecture and long-standing city character.

mustVisit.name: National Museum of Ethiopia
mustVisit.description: Key museum with archaeological and cultural collections, including Lucy.

mustVisit.name: African Union Headquarters area
mustVisit.description: Symbolic district reflecting Addis’s continental diplomatic role.

mustVisit.name: Merkato
mustVisit.description: Massive open-air market for a full sensory dive into city commerce.

destinations.visaInfo: eVisa typically required for U.S. citizen visiting Ethiopia; verify current entry rules before travel.
destinations.ratingNotes: African Union capital; Black-majority; pan-African significance

destinations.tagline: Europe but warmer — in every sense
destinations.vibe: Europe but warmer — in every sense. Strong Afro-Portuguese community.
destinations.safetyNotes: Lisbon is generally safe and welcoming. Portugal has a complex colonial history with Africa that creates a nuanced dynamic — there is a significant Cape Verdean, Angolan, and Mozambican community here, so you'll feel more at home than in many European cities. Occasional microaggressions exist but overt racism is uncommon. Mouraria neighborhood has a beautiful multicultural energy.

neighborhoods.name: Mouraria
neighborhoods.description: Lisbon's most multicultural neighborhood — historic Moorish quarter with Cape Verdean restaurants and fado bars.

neighborhoods.name: Alfama
neighborhoods.description: The oldest neighborhood — steep hills, tiled houses, fado music. Beautiful chaos.

neighborhoods.name: Chiado
neighborhoods.description: Upscale shopping, great cafes, central location. Very walkable.

neighborhoods.name: Belem
neighborhoods.description: Where the Age of Exploration began. Museums, monuments, and the best pasteis de nata.

mustVisit.name: Mouraria Walking Tour
mustVisit.description: Best way to understand Lisbon's African diaspora connection. Seek out local guides from the community.

mustVisit.name: Cape Verdean Restaurants
mustVisit.description: Mouraria has several Cape Verdean restaurants — cachupa (stew) is a must. Ask locals for current favorites.

mustVisit.name: MAAT Museum of Art, Architecture and Technology
mustVisit.description: World-class contemporary museum on the waterfront. Often features African and diaspora artists.

mustVisit.name: Fado in Alfama
mustVisit.description: Fado is Portugal's soul music — melancholic and beautiful. Experience it live in a small tasca in Alfama, not a tourist trap.

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens (Schengen — 90 days max)
destinations.ratingNotes: Afro-Portuguese community in Mouraria; warm culture; some racism

destinations.tagline: Central High School — where nine Black students desegregated in 1957 with federal troops — is a N...
destinations.vibe: Central High School — where nine Black students desegregated in 1957 with federal troops — is a National Historic Site. Little Rock's civil rights history is essential American history.
destinations.safetyNotes: Downtown Little Rock and Central High School area are safe for visitors. Generally welcoming city with strong civil rights heritage.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown Little Rock
neighborhoods.description: Clinton Presidential Library, Riverfront Park, River Market District

neighborhoods.name: Central High Historic District
neighborhoods.description: Desegregation crisis of 1957 — one of the defining moments of the Civil Rights Movement

mustVisit.name: Little Rock Central High School National Historic Site
mustVisit.description: The 1957 desegregation crisis that required Eisenhower to deploy the 101st Airborne — visitors center across the street

mustVisit.name: Mosaic Templars Cultural Center
mustVisit.description: Museum documenting the history of the Mosaic Templars of America — a significant African American fraternal organization

mustVisit.name: Arkansas Civil Rights History Tour
mustVisit.description: State has created an excellent self-guided tour of civil rights sites

mustVisit.name: Little Rock Nine Monument
mustVisit.description: Bronze statues outside the Arkansas State Capitol honoring the Little Rock Nine

mustVisit.name: Clinton Presidential Center
mustVisit.description: Presidential library with significant civil rights policy exhibits

mustVisit.name: Community Bakery
mustVisit.description: Long-running local favorite breakfast and lunch spot

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens
destinations.ratingNotes: Central High desegregation; Arkansas Black community

destinations.tagline: Black British culture is one of the greatest gifts to the world
destinations.vibe: Black British culture — from Brixton to Notting Hill — has shaped the entire world.
destinations.safetyNotes: London is one of the most diverse and generally safe major cities in the world. Brixton, Peckham, Hackney, and Tottenham are thriving Black British cultural hubs. Tourists should take standard precautions: be alert on the Tube and crowded areas for pickpockets. The city's diversity means you'll find community and comfort in most neighborhoods.

neighborhoods.name: Brixton
neighborhoods.description: The heartbeat of Black British culture — Windrush generation history, Brixton Market, incredible food, and Afrobeats/grime venues.

neighborhoods.name: Notting Hill
neighborhoods.description: Home of the Notting Hill Carnival — the largest street festival in Europe. Caribbean culture, colorful houses, and great restaurants.

neighborhoods.name: Peckham
neighborhoods.description: South London's coolest neighborhood — Nigerian and Ghanaian restaurants, rooftop bars, and a thriving creative Black scene.

neighborhoods.name: Shoreditch
neighborhoods.description: Street art, global cuisine, and London's arts and tech hub. Diverse and energetic with great nightlife.

mustVisit.name: Brixton Market
mustVisit.description: A multi-cultural food market under Victorian arches — Afro-Caribbean ingredients, restaurants, and the pulse of Black London.
mustVisit.address: Electric Ave & Brixton Village, London SW9

mustVisit.name: Notting Hill Carnival
mustVisit.description: Europe's largest street festival (August Bank Holiday). Two million people, Caribbean sound systems, jerk chicken, and costumes. Unmissable.

mustVisit.name: Museum of the Home
mustVisit.description: Recently reinterpreted museum in Hackney exploring how race and colonialism shaped the idea of 'home.' Thoughtfully done.

mustVisit.name: Frank's Cafe Peckham Rooftop
mustVisit.description: Legendary seasonal rooftop bar with panoramic south London views. The Peckham scene in one place.

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens (up to 6 months as visitor)
destinations.ratingNotes: Large Black British community; police stop-and-search issue; welcoming

destinations.tagline: Entertainment capital with a massive Black creative community. Leimert Park is the cultural heart...
destinations.vibe: Entertainment capital with a massive Black creative community. Leimert Park is the cultural heart of Black LA. Black Hollywood history, Roscoe's, and a booming Black-owned restaurant scene make LA essential.
destinations.safetyNotes: LA is vast and varies wildly by area. Leimert Park, Crenshaw, and Baldwin Hills are safe Black cultural hubs. Hollywood, Santa Monica, and Beverly Hills are tourist-friendly. Traffic is a major factor. Some skid row areas require awareness.

neighborhoods.name: Leimert Park
neighborhoods.description: Cultural heart of Black Los Angeles, jazz, arts, Afrocentric businesses

neighborhoods.name: Crenshaw
neighborhoods.description: Nipsey Hussle's home ground, Marathon Clothing, Destination Crenshaw outdoor art

neighborhoods.name: Baldwin Hills / Ladera Heights
neighborhoods.description: Known as 'Black Beverly Hills' — affluent Black community

neighborhoods.name: Inglewood
neighborhoods.description: SoFi Stadium, rising Black-owned businesses, historic community

mustVisit.name: California African American Museum
mustVisit.description: Free museum in Exposition Park celebrating Black art, history, and culture in California

mustVisit.name: Destination Crenshaw
mustVisit.description: 1.3-mile outdoor museum celebrating Black LA culture along Crenshaw Blvd

mustVisit.name: Roscoe's House of Chicken and Waffles
mustVisit.description: LA institution since 1975 — the definitive chicken and waffles experience

mustVisit.name: Marathon Clothing / Nipsey Hussle Mural
mustVisit.description: Tribute to Nipsey Hussle in Crenshaw, Marathon Store still operates

mustVisit.name: Museum of the African Diaspora (MOAD)
mustVisit.description: San Francisco sister institution — also worth visiting for Bay Area trips

mustVisit.name: Taste of Soul Festival
mustVisit.description: Annual October festival on Crenshaw — largest African American street festival on the West Coast

destinations.bestTimeToVisit: March-May, September-November (avoid summer heat and peak crowds)
destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens
destinations.ratingNotes: Large Black community; LAPD issues; Leimert Park hub

destinations.tagline: Muhammad Ali's birthplace. Breonna Taylor's death made Louisville internationally known. The Muha...
destinations.vibe: Muhammad Ali's birthplace. Breonna Taylor's death made Louisville internationally known. The Muhammad Ali Center is one of the best museums in America. Kentucky Derby is a major Black cultural event.
destinations.safetyNotes: Louisville is generally safe in tourist areas. The NuLu district, Cherokee Triangle, and Highlands are welcoming. Some areas require standard urban awareness.

neighborhoods.name: West Louisville
neighborhoods.description: Muhammad Ali's birthplace neighborhood — Portland and California neighborhoods

neighborhoods.name: NuLu
neighborhoods.description: New Louisville arts district with diverse food and galleries

neighborhoods.name: Highland/Bardstown Road
neighborhoods.description: Louisville's most diverse dining and arts district

mustVisit.name: Muhammad Ali Center
mustVisit.description: One of America's great museums — Ali's life, legacy, and global humanitarian impact. Profoundly moving

mustVisit.name: Muhammad Ali's Birthplace
mustVisit.description: Ali's childhood home on Grand Ave — 716 Grand Avenue, West Louisville

mustVisit.name: Churchill Downs / Kentucky Derby Museum
mustVisit.description: Black jockeys won 15 of first 28 Derbies — important forgotten history at the museum

mustVisit.name: Breonna Taylor memorial sites
mustVisit.description: Jefferson Square Park memorial — community tribute to Breonna Taylor

mustVisit.name: Louisville Slugger Museum & Factory
mustVisit.description: Home of the famous baseball bat — interesting for baseball history fans

mustVisit.name: 732 Social (Black-owned lounge)
mustVisit.description: Popular Black-owned lounge in Louisville's West End

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens
destinations.ratingNotes: Muhammad Ali birthplace; Breonna Taylor legacy; LMPD issues

destinations.tagline: Bavarian tradition with clean streets and polished comfort.
destinations.vibe: Munich is organized, affluent, and easiergoing than its reputation suggests, especially if you like museums, parks, and day trips. For Black travelers, it can feel less diverse than Berlin, but the city is usually smooth to navigate and rarely chaotic.
destinations.safetyNotes: Generally very safe, with pickpocketing around train stations and large festivals the most common issue. Because Munich can feel socially conservative, some Black travelers may notice stares more than direct confrontation.

neighborhoods.name: Altstadt-Lehel
neighborhoods.description: Historic center with landmarks, shopping, and classic Munich sights.

neighborhoods.name: Schwabing
neighborhoods.description: Lively district near the English Garden with cafés and culture.

neighborhoods.name: Glockenbachviertel
neighborhoods.description: Stylish area with nightlife, boutiques, and a more modern city feel.

mustVisit.name: Marienplatz
mustVisit.description: Central square and classic starting point for the city.

mustVisit.name: English Garden
mustVisit.description: Huge urban park ideal for long walks and relaxed summer days.

mustVisit.name: Pinakothek der Moderne
mustVisit.description: Major museum for modern art, design, and architecture.

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for U.S. citizen for short tourist stays in Germany/Schengen.
destinations.ratingNotes: Conservative Bavaria; some racism documented; limited Black community

destinations.tagline: Skyline glam, Afro-Caribbean influence, and old-city contrast
destinations.vibe: Panama City feels like a real crossroads, part financial capital, part historic port, part nightlife playground. For Black travelers, it can be a very strong city break because Afro-Caribbean influence, great food, and big-city convenience all sit next to easy access to history and day trips.
destinations.safetyNotes: Panama City is generally straightforward for visitors in established neighborhoods like Casco Viejo, Obarrio, and Punta Pacifica, but some areas are best avoided without local knowledge. Use the same city caution you would elsewhere at night, be selective about neighborhoods, and do not treat the city as uniformly tourist-friendly block to block.

neighborhoods.name: Casco Viejo
neighborhoods.description: Historic quarter packed with bars, hotels, rooftops, and restored architecture.

neighborhoods.name: Obarrio
neighborhoods.description: Central business-and-dining district with practical access and nightlife.

neighborhoods.name: Cinta Costera / Bella Vista
neighborhoods.description: Bayfront zone for skyline views, walking, and urban convenience.

mustVisit.name: Panama Canal
mustVisit.description: Essential landmark that anchors the city’s global identity.

mustVisit.name: Biomuseo
mustVisit.description: Visually striking museum connecting Panama’s ecology and history.

mustVisit.name: Casco Viejo
mustVisit.description: Neighborhood-scale experience for architecture, cocktails, and street life.

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizen for short tourist stays

destinations.tagline: African magic — ancient, sensory, and absolutely unforgettable
destinations.vibe: Ancient African magic — riads, souks, gnawa music, and the pulse of a city that has never stopped.
destinations.safetyNotes: Marrakech is generally safe for tourists. The medina can feel overwhelming but is walkable with confidence. Beware of unofficial guides who will offer to help and then demand payment — politely decline. As a Black visitor you may experience some staring, but Morocco has a deep Saharan African heritage and connection. The riads and tourist areas are very safe.

neighborhoods.name: Medina (Old City)
neighborhoods.description: A UNESCO World Heritage labyrinth of souks, mosques, and riads. Jemaa el-Fna square is the beating heart.

neighborhoods.name: Jemaa el-Fna
neighborhoods.description: The world's greatest public square — storytellers, snake charmers, musicians, and food stalls that transform at night.

neighborhoods.name: Gueliz (New City)
neighborhoods.description: Marrakech's modern French-era district — upscale restaurants, art galleries, and European-style cafes.

neighborhoods.name: Mellah (Jewish Quarter)
neighborhoods.description: Historic Jewish quarter adjacent to the medina — beautiful synagogues, silver souks, and deep multi-faith history.

mustVisit.name: Jardin Majorelle
mustVisit.description: Stunning cobalt-blue botanical garden — bought and restored by Yves Saint Laurent. The Berber Museum inside is excellent.

mustVisit.name: Jemaa el-Fna at Night
mustVisit.description: When the sun sets, the square transforms into a massive open-air food and entertainment festival. One of the great spectacles on Earth.

mustVisit.name: Gnawa Music Experience
mustVisit.description: Gnawa is an ancient sub-Saharan African spiritual music tradition that survived in Morocco. Seek out a lila ceremony or Gnawa musician in the medina.

mustVisit.name: Ben Youssef Medersa
mustVisit.description: Morocco's largest Quranic school — astonishingly beautiful geometric tiling, carved cedar, and stucco. A masterpiece of Islamic architecture.

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens (up to 90 days)
destinations.ratingNotes: Sub-Saharan Black travelers report harassment; colorism issues

destinations.tagline: The most iconic Black vacation destination in America. Oak Bluffs has been a Black summer resort ...
destinations.vibe: The most iconic Black vacation destination in America. Oak Bluffs has been a Black summer resort since the 1880s. The Obama family vacations here. Every August the island fills with Black professionals, celebrities, and families.
destinations.safetyNotes: Martha's Vineyard is extremely safe and welcoming. One of the most beloved Black vacation spots in America. The island is small and intimate — expect to see people you know.

neighborhoods.name: Oak Bluffs
neighborhoods.description: The Inkwell beach, historically Black gingerbread cottages, NAACP history, Dreamland building

neighborhoods.name: Edgartown
neighborhoods.description: Whaling history, luxury hotels, Chappaquiddick

neighborhoods.name: Vineyard Haven
neighborhoods.description: Where the ferry arrives from Cape Cod — main entry point

mustVisit.name: The Inkwell Beach (Oak Bluffs)
mustVisit.description: The legendary historically Black beach in Oak Bluffs — the original Black beach resort

mustVisit.name: NAACP Oak Bluffs Branch
mustVisit.description: One of the oldest NAACP branches — African Americans have vacationed here since the 1880s

mustVisit.name: Lola's Southern Seafood
mustVisit.description: Oak Bluffs institution — the place to be seen in Black MV

mustVisit.name: Dreamland (Lincoln Center of the Vineyard)
mustVisit.description: Former roller skating rink that was a center of Black social life in Oak Bluffs

mustVisit.name: Flying Horses Carousel
mustVisit.description: Oldest platform merry-go-round in the US — Oak Bluffs centerpiece

mustVisit.name: Illumination Night (Oak Bluffs)
mustVisit.description: Annual cottage lantern lighting — magical summer tradition

destinations.bestTimeToVisit: July-August (peak season — expensive but essential), June and September for more affordable visits
destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens

destinations.tagline: Birthplace of the blues, soul, and rock and roll — built by Black musicians. The National Civil R...
destinations.vibe: Birthplace of the blues, soul, and rock and roll — built by Black musicians. The National Civil Rights Museum at the Lorraine Motel is one of America's most important historic sites.
destinations.safetyNotes: Memphis has elevated crime rates. Beale Street, South Main Arts District, and Midtown are tourist-friendly. The National Civil Rights Museum area is safe. Exercise caution in other parts of the city.

neighborhoods.name: Beale Street
neighborhoods.description: Blues capital of the world — W.C. Handy, B.B. King all played here

neighborhoods.name: South Main Arts District
neighborhoods.description: National Civil Rights Museum, trolley cars, galleries and restaurants

neighborhoods.name: Orange Mound
neighborhoods.description: One of the first neighborhoods in the US built by and for African Americans

neighborhoods.name: Midtown
neighborhoods.description: Cooper-Young restaurant district, Sun Studio nearby

mustVisit.name: National Civil Rights Museum at the Lorraine Motel
mustVisit.description: Where MLK was assassinated — one of the most moving and important museums in America

mustVisit.name: Blues Hall of Fame Museum
mustVisit.description: Celebrates the African American roots of the blues

mustVisit.name: Sun Studio
mustVisit.description: Where Elvis recorded his first sessions — but also where Black artists like BB King laid the foundation

mustVisit.name: Gus's World Famous Fried Chicken
mustVisit.description: Legendary Memphis fried chicken — pilgrimage-worthy

mustVisit.name: Graceland
mustVisit.description: Elvis's home — important for understanding how Black music shaped mainstream culture

mustVisit.name: Stax Museum of American Soul Music
mustVisit.description: Where Otis Redding, Isaac Hayes, and the Staple Singers recorded — soul music's ground zero

destinations.bestTimeToVisit: March-May, October-November (avoid hot humid summers), Memphis in May festival
destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens
destinations.ratingNotes: Soul music capital; majority-Black; Deep South community warmth

destinations.tagline: Fort Lauderdale is less expensive than Miami with beautiful beaches. Lauderdale's Black community...
destinations.vibe: Fort Lauderdale is less expensive than Miami with beautiful beaches. Lauderdale's Black community in Roosevelt Gardens and the annual Fort Lauderdale Pride, plus easy access to Miami, make it popular with Black travelers.
destinations.safetyNotes: Fort Lauderdale's beach areas and Las Olas Blvd are very safe. Some inland neighborhoods require standard awareness. Generally a very tourist-friendly destination.

neighborhoods.name: Las Olas Blvd / Riverwalk
neighborhoods.description: Fort Lauderdale's premiere dining and shopping street along the New River

neighborhoods.name: Sistrunk Corridor
neighborhoods.description: Historic African American Fort Lauderdale — community organizations and cultural events

mustVisit.name: Fort Lauderdale Beach
mustVisit.description: Beautiful wide beach with the famous A1A coastal highway

mustVisit.name: NSU Art Museum Fort Lauderdale
mustVisit.description: World-class art museum with significant African American art collection

mustVisit.name: Everglades National Park (45 min)
mustVisit.description: America's largest subtropical wilderness — airboat rides, alligators, manatees

mustVisit.name: Las Olas Boulevard
mustVisit.description: Fort Lauderdale's best dining and shopping street — diverse restaurants and galleries

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens
destinations.ratingNotes: Less expensive than Miami; Black community present

destinations.tagline: Where the Caribbean meets the American dream
destinations.vibe: Where the Caribbean meets the American dream — Afro-Caribbean culture, Black luxury, and endless sun.
destinations.safetyNotes: Miami is very safe in tourist areas. South Beach, Wynwood, Brickell, and the Design District are all heavily policed and well-lit. Little Haiti and Liberty City are authentic Black neighborhoods worth visiting during the day. Uber is widely available. Miami is a Black-luxury destination — you will absolutely see and feel that energy.

neighborhoods.name: Little Haiti
neighborhoods.description: Miami's vibrant Haitian community — Haitian restaurants, Caribbean art, botanicas, and a proud cultural identity.

neighborhoods.name: Wynwood
neighborhoods.description: Street art capital of Miami. Black artists are heavily featured. Great galleries, restaurants, and the famous Wynwood Walls.

neighborhoods.name: South Beach
neighborhoods.description: Art Deco architecture, beach clubs, and the center of Miami's luxury nightlife scene.

neighborhoods.name: Overtown
neighborhoods.description: Historic Black neighborhood once called the 'Harlem of the South' — deep roots and growing cultural revival.

mustVisit.name: Art Basel Miami
mustVisit.description: December's Art Basel brings the global Black art world to Miami. One of the most important cultural events of the year.
mustVisit.address: Miami Beach Convention Center, Miami Beach, FL

mustVisit.name: Tap Tap Haitian Restaurant
mustVisit.description: Legendary Little Haiti restaurant with incredible Haitian food, rum punch, and vibrant Caribbean art covering every wall.

mustVisit.name: HistoryMiami Museum
mustVisit.description: Excellent exhibits on Miami's multicultural history including Afro-Caribbean and Black Floridian stories.

mustVisit.name: Virginia Key Beach Park
mustVisit.description: Miami's historic 'Black beach' — the only Miami beach Black residents could access during segregation. Now a beautiful park with deep meaning.

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens
destinations.ratingNotes: Caribbean Black community; colorism can be issue; MPD concerns

destinations.tagline: Milwaukee's Bronzeville district is one of the Midwest's most significant Black heritage areas. A...
destinations.vibe: Milwaukee's Bronzeville district is one of the Midwest's most significant Black heritage areas. America's Black Holocaust Museum reopened in 2021. Growing Black art scene and excellent Friday Night Fish Fry culture.
destinations.safetyNotes: Milwaukee has elevated crime in some areas. Brady Street, Historic Third Ward, and the East Side are safe for visitors. Bronzeville is safe during the day. Research specific neighborhoods.

neighborhoods.name: Bronzeville
neighborhoods.description: Milwaukee's historic Black neighborhood — America's Black Holocaust Museum, Bronzeville Collective

neighborhoods.name: Historic Third Ward
neighborhoods.description: Milwaukee's premier arts and dining district

neighborhoods.name: Walker's Point
neighborhoods.description: Milwaukee's most diverse neighborhood with growing arts scene

mustVisit.name: America's Black Holocaust Museum
mustVisit.description: Reopened 2021 — founded by the only known survivor of a lynching. Powerful documentation of anti-Black violence in America

mustVisit.name: Bronzeville Collective MKE
mustVisit.description: Black and Brown artisan marketplace — 25+ local brands in the historic Bronzeville neighborhood

mustVisit.name: Milwaukee Art Museum
mustVisit.description: Stunning Santiago Calatrava-designed museum on Lake Michigan

mustVisit.name: Friday Night Fish Fry
mustVisit.description: Milwaukee tradition — fried perch, coleslaw, potato pancakes every Friday at local taverns

mustVisit.name: Summerfest
mustVisit.description: World's largest music festival — 11 days on the lakefront with major Black artists

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens
destinations.ratingNotes: Bronzeville heritage; significant Black community; midwestern

destinations.tagline: Minneapolis gained global attention after George Floyd's murder in 2020. The city has a significa...
destinations.vibe: Minneapolis gained global attention after George Floyd's murder in 2020. The city has a significant Black community on the North Side and in the Rondo neighborhood (St. Paul). Prince's hometown and a vibrant Black arts scene.
destinations.safetyNotes: Minneapolis has elevated crime in some areas post-2020. The North Loop, Uptown, and Lake District are very safe. North Minneapolis community areas require more awareness. Generally a welcoming, progressive city.

neighborhoods.name: North Minneapolis
neighborhoods.description: Heart of the Black community — George Floyd murals, cultural resilience

neighborhoods.name: Rondo Neighborhood (St. Paul)
neighborhoods.description: St. Paul's historic Black community — Rondo was destroyed by I-94 construction in the 1950s

neighborhoods.name: Uptown
neighborhoods.description: Minneapolis's trendiest neighborhood around Lakes Calhoun and Harriet

mustVisit.name: George Floyd Square
mustVisit.description: 38th and Chicago — the memorial to George Floyd transformed into a community-led sacred space

mustVisit.name: Prince's Paisley Park
mustVisit.description: Prince's legendary recording complex in Chanhassen — tours available year-round

mustVisit.name: Minnesota African American Heritage Museum
mustVisit.description: Documents the history of African Americans in Minnesota

mustVisit.name: Minneapolis Chain of Lakes
mustVisit.description: Beautiful urban lake system — biking, kayaking, and outdoor recreation

mustVisit.name: Midtown Global Market
mustVisit.description: Diverse food hall in a historic Sears building — African, Latino, Asian vendors

destinations.bestTimeToVisit: June-September (avoid brutal winters), Mall of America year-round
destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens
destinations.ratingNotes: George Floyd hometown; MPD issues serious; Black community present

destinations.tagline: Mobile invented Mardi Gras in America — celebrating since 1703, 15 years before New Orleans. Mobi...
destinations.vibe: Mobile invented Mardi Gras in America — celebrating since 1703, 15 years before New Orleans. Mobile's African American Mardi Gras tradition is the original and deeply cultural. The city has significant civil rights history.
destinations.safetyNotes: Mobile is a mid-size Alabama port city. Downtown and the historic district are safe for visitors. Some areas require standard urban awareness.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown Mobile / Oakleigh Garden District
neighborhoods.description: Beautiful antebellum architecture — where American Mardi Gras was born

neighborhoods.name: Africatown
neighborhoods.description: Founded by the last Africans brought to the US on the Clotilda slave ship (1860) — the Clotilda was recently found

mustVisit.description: Documents the story of the Clotilda — the last known slave ship to the US, whose survivors founded this community

mustVisit.name: Clotilda Discovery Exhibit
mustVisit.description: The wreck of the Clotilda was discovered in 2019 — ongoing archaeological work is critical Black history

mustVisit.name: Mobile Mardi Gras
mustVisit.description: America's original Mardi Gras — Black Mystic krewe has marched since 1938

mustVisit.name: USS Alabama Battleship Memorial Park
mustVisit.description: WWII battleship with significant Black military history on display

mustVisit.name: Fort Conde
mustVisit.description: Reconstruction of French colonial fort — Mobile's founding as a colonial city

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens
destinations.ratingNotes: Invented Mardi Gras; Africatown nearby; Black history significant

destinations.tagline: Cradle of the Civil Rights Movement. The National Memorial for Peace and Justice (lynching memori...
destinations.vibe: Cradle of the Civil Rights Movement. The National Memorial for Peace and Justice (lynching memorial) and Legacy Museum are transformative experiences. Rosa Parks refused to give up her seat here. A pilgrimage destination.
destinations.safetyNotes: Downtown Montgomery is safe for tourists, especially around the civil rights sites. A relatively small city — easy to navigate.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown Montgomery
neighborhoods.description: State capital, civil rights museums, Dexter Avenue Baptist Church

neighborhoods.name: West Montgomery
neighborhoods.description: Historic African American neighborhood

neighborhoods.name: Cloverdale / Midtown
neighborhoods.description: Gentrifying historic neighborhood with restaurants

mustVisit.name: National Memorial for Peace and Justice (Lynching Memorial)
mustVisit.description: Bryan Stevenson's transformative memorial to 4,000+ lynching victims — one of the most powerful sites in America

mustVisit.name: Legacy Museum
mustVisit.description: Equal Justice Initiative's museum documenting slavery to mass incarceration — essential viewing

mustVisit.name: Dexter Avenue King Memorial Baptist Church
mustVisit.description: MLK's church — where the Montgomery Bus Boycott was organized

mustVisit.name: Rosa Parks Museum
mustVisit.description: Tells the story of Rosa Parks and the Bus Boycott

mustVisit.name: Civil Rights Memorial Center
mustVisit.description: Maya Lin-designed memorial to civil rights martyrs

mustVisit.name: Chris' Hot Dogs
mustVisit.description: Historic Montgomery institution that served Booker T. Washington and FDR

destinations.bestTimeToVisit: March-May (civil rights anniversary events in April), September-November
destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens
destinations.ratingNotes: Civil rights cradle; EJI/Bryan Stevenson; significant pilgrimage

destinations.tagline: Myrtle Beach is hugely popular with Black travelers, especially during Black Bike Week (Memorial ...
destinations.vibe: Myrtle Beach is hugely popular with Black travelers, especially during Black Bike Week (Memorial Day weekend) and Atlantic Beach's historical significance. Atlantic Beach — 'the Black Pearl' — is adjacent.
destinations.safetyNotes: Myrtle Beach is generally safe in tourist areas. Ocean Drive and Atlantic Beach are accessible. Some tension around Black Bike Week historically, but has improved.

neighborhoods.name: Atlantic Beach
neighborhoods.description: One of the last surviving Black beach communities from the Jim Crow era — 'Black Pearl of the Strand'

neighborhoods.name: Ocean Drive / North Myrtle Beach
neighborhoods.description: Home of the Shag dance — important Black music crossover history

neighborhoods.name: Broadway at the Beach
neighborhoods.description: Major entertainment complex popular with families

mustVisit.name: Atlantic Beach (The Black Pearl)
mustVisit.description: Historic Black beach resort town that refused to integrate — now celebrated as a heritage site

mustVisit.name: Black Bike Week (Memorial Day)
mustVisit.description: One of the largest annual gatherings of Black motorcyclists in America

mustVisit.name: Myrtle Beach State Park
mustVisit.description: Pristine stretch of beach with maritime forest — a beautiful contrast to commercial strip

mustVisit.name: The Strip
mustVisit.description: Amusement parks, mini golf, seafood restaurants along Ocean Blvd

mustVisit.name: Local seafood restaurants
mustVisit.description: Calabash-style fried seafood is a Myrtle Beach specialty

destinations.bestTimeToVisit: April-October (beach season), Black Bike Week (Memorial Day), Atlantic Beach Bikefest (May)
destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens
destinations.ratingNotes: Black Bike Week tradition; beach access; limited year-round BOP

destinations.tagline: A deep Yoruba city of books, markets, and cultural memory.
destinations.vibe: Ibadan feels grounded in history rather than hype—big, sprawling, intellectual, and unmistakably Yoruba. For Black travelers, it can be especially meaningful if you like university energy, local food, and culture that feels lived rather than packaged.
destinations.safetyNotes: More about practical caution than tourist polish: traffic, petty crime, and uneven infrastructure can wear you down if you move loosely. Travel with a plan, limit night wandering, and lean on local guidance for markets and outer districts.

neighborhoods.name: Bodija
neighborhoods.description: Established residential-commercial area with eateries, schools, and a more familiar pace.

neighborhoods.name: Mokola
neighborhoods.description: Busy central district known for shopping, transport links, and street energy.

neighborhoods.name: UI/Agbowo
neighborhoods.description: University-adjacent zone with student life, bookstores, and affordable food.

mustVisit.name: University of Ibadan Zoological Garden
mustVisit.description: Longstanding campus attraction tied to the city’s academic identity.

mustVisit.name: Bower’s Tower
mustVisit.description: Historic lookout point with sweeping views over the city.

mustVisit.name: Mapo Hall
mustVisit.description: Colonial-era civic landmark tied to Ibadan’s political and urban history.

destinations.visaInfo: Visa or eVisa generally required for U.S. citizen; verify current Nigeria entry rules before travel.
destinations.ratingNotes: Deep Yoruba city; Black-majority; all businesses Black-owned

destinations.tagline: Track-town spirit with artsy Pacific Northwest calm
destinations.vibe: Eugene is a mellow city that blends college-town life, outdoor access, and a handmade local feel. For a Black traveler, it is not a diaspora-heavy destination, but it can still be a comfortable stop if you want markets, trails, bookstores, and a lower-pressure Oregon pace.
destinations.safetyNotes: Eugene is generally easy for visitors, particularly around downtown, campus, and the 5th Street area. The main thing is to keep standard awareness around isolated paths at night and to treat it like a smaller city where activity can thin out quickly after business hours.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown Eugene
neighborhoods.description: Compact center with restaurants, arts venues, and weekend market activity.

neighborhoods.name: Whiteaker
neighborhoods.description: Creative neighborhood with breweries, murals, and a more alternative edge.

neighborhoods.name: University District
neighborhoods.description: Campus-adjacent zone with coffee shops and steady daytime activity.

mustVisit.name: Jordan Schnitzer Museum of Art
mustVisit.description: Strong university museum that adds real culture to a Eugene stay.

mustVisit.name: 5th Street Public Market
mustVisit.description: Popular mixed-use stop for shopping, food, and easy trip planning.

mustVisit.name: Skinner Butte Park
mustVisit.description: Quick nature escape with views and access to the riverfront.

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizen
destinations.ratingNotes: Oregon; Oregon's Black exclusion laws legacy; limited community

destinations.tagline: Where Black history, mountain access, and city energy come together
destinations.vibe: Denver works well for Black travelers because it offers both urban activity and real African American heritage, especially if you spend time in Five Points. It can feel expensive and fast-changing, but it still has cultural anchors that make it more than a base camp for outdoorsy people.
destinations.safetyNotes: Denver is one of the easier big cities in this batch to navigate, especially in central districts, but property crime and uneven blocks are real concerns. Stay alert in nightlife areas, use normal city caution, and do not assume every trendy zone is equally comfortable after dark.

neighborhoods.name: Five Points
neighborhoods.description: Historic Black neighborhood known as the Harlem of the West.

neighborhoods.name: RiNo
neighborhoods.description: Art-heavy district with murals, breweries, and nightlife.

neighborhoods.name: LoDo
neighborhoods.description: Downtown warehouse district with hotels, restaurants, and transit access.

mustVisit.name: Black American West Museum
mustVisit.description: Must-visit museum centering Black western history and Denver's deeper past.

mustVisit.name: Welton Street Historic District
mustVisit.description: Historic Five Points corridor tied to Denver's Black commerce and jazz legacy.

mustVisit.name: Denver Museum of Nature & Science
mustVisit.description: Reliable major museum stop that broadens a Denver itinerary beyond nightlife.

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizen
destinations.ratingNotes: 5 Points jazz heritage; growing Black community; Colorado

destinations.tagline: Safari, skyline, and startup energy
destinations.vibe: Safari, skyline, and startup energy — Nairobi is East Africa's most dynamic city.
destinations.safetyNotes: Nairobi has a reputation but is very manageable with smart habits. Westlands, Karen, Kilimani, and the CBD are safe for tourists. Avoid walking at night in the CBD — take Ubers everywhere after dark. Nairobi is increasingly cosmopolitan and home to a growing Black expat and tech community. Kenyans are genuinely friendly and welcoming to visitors.

neighborhoods.name: Westlands
neighborhoods.description: Nairobi's entertainment hub — rooftop bars, restaurants, live music venues, and vibrant nightlife.

neighborhoods.name: Karen
neighborhoods.description: Leafy, upscale suburb — beautiful gardens, boutique shopping, and safari lodges nearby.

neighborhoods.name: Kilimani
neighborhoods.description: Residential and restaurant district. Great brunch spots, coffee shops, and the cool Nairobi scene.

neighborhoods.name: Maasai Market
neighborhoods.description: The best place to buy authentic African crafts, jewelry, and textiles from Maasai artisans.

mustVisit.name: Nairobi National Park
mustVisit.description: The only game reserve bordering a capital city in the world. You can see lions with a city skyline backdrop. Extraordinary.

mustVisit.name: Giraffe Centre
mustVisit.description: Hand-feed endangered Rothschild giraffes at this beloved Nairobi institution. Essential experience.

mustVisit.name: Nairobi National Museum
mustVisit.description: Excellent collection of Kenyan history, natural history, and contemporary African art.

mustVisit.name: Carnivore Restaurant
mustVisit.description: Legendary Nairobi institution — an all-you-can-eat African meat feast. A rite of passage for Nairobi visitors.

destinations.visaInfo: e-Visa required. Apply online at evisa.go.ke. US citizens eligible.
destinations.ratingNotes: Welcoming to diaspora; police corruption moderate risk

destinations.tagline: Beyond country music, Nashville has deep Black history — Fisk University, the Jubilee Singers, an...
destinations.vibe: Beyond country music, Nashville has deep Black history — Fisk University, the Jubilee Singers, and a booming Black food scene. Black travelers are discovering Nashville's Hot Chicken culture and vibrant north Nashville community.
destinations.safetyNotes: Nashville is generally safe in tourist areas. Broadway, Germantown, East Nashville, and North Nashville are all accessible. The rapid gentrification is changing some neighborhoods.

neighborhoods.description: Historic Black Nashville — Fisk University, Tennessee State University, civil rights movement hub

neighborhoods.name: East Nashville
neighborhoods.description: Trendy neighborhood with Black-owned restaurants and creative community

neighborhoods.name: Germantown
neighborhoods.description: Walkable neighborhood near downtown with great restaurant scene

neighborhoods.name: Downtown / Broadway
neighborhoods.description: Honky tonk row — but also historic with deep Black music connections

mustVisit.name: Fisk University and Jubilee Hall
mustVisit.description: HBCU that sent the Jubilee Singers around the world — stunning Gothic building built with proceeds

mustVisit.description: Opened 2021 — celebrates the African American roots of all American music genres

mustVisit.name: Prince's Hot Chicken Shack

mustVisit.name: Tennessee State Museum
mustVisit.description: Excellent exhibits on Tennessee African American history

mustVisit.name: Jefferson Street

mustVisit.name: Biscuit Love
