mustVisit.address: St. Thomas / St. Croix / St. John, US Virgin Islands

mustVisit.name: Cruzan Rum Distillery (St. Croix)
mustVisit.description: History of rum and sugar in the Caribbean — important context for understanding plantation economy
mustVisit.address: St. Thomas / St. Croix / St. John, US Virgin Islands

destinations.bestTimeToVisit: December-April (dry season), avoid hurricane season June-November
destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens
destinations.ratingNotes: Black-majority USVI; Afro-Caribbean heritage strong

destinations.tagline: Storybook streets with beer-hall energy and easy value.
destinations.vibe: Prague is beautiful almost to the point of disbelief, but it also stays accessible for travelers who do not want a luxury budget. For Black travelers, it can feel welcoming enough for a short trip, though the city is less diverse and some visitors report curiosity that can feel awkward.
destinations.safetyNotes: Generally safe, with petty theft and tourist scams the main issue in crowded central areas. Stay alert around Old Town, Charles Bridge, and transit chokepoints, especially at night and during peak season.

neighborhoods.name: Staré Město
neighborhoods.description: Historic center with major landmarks and constant tourist flow.

neighborhoods.name: Vinohrady
neighborhoods.description: Leafier district with cafés, wine bars, and a more local pace.

neighborhoods.name: Holešovice
neighborhoods.description: Former industrial area turned creative hub with galleries and nightlife.

mustVisit.name: Prague Castle
mustVisit.description: Hilltop castle complex overlooking the city.

mustVisit.name: Old Town Square
mustVisit.description: The city’s famed medieval square and gathering point.

mustVisit.name: DOX Centre for Contemporary Art
mustVisit.description: Leading contemporary art venue in a converted industrial setting.

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for U.S. citizen for short tourist stays in Czechia/Schengen.
destinations.ratingNotes: Eastern Europe; documented Black traveler discrimination; very limited BOP

destinations.tagline: Hill Country water fun with an easy weekend pace
destinations.vibe: New Braunfels works when you want a playful Texas getaway centered on rivers, live music, and a small-town core with tourism infrastructure. From a Black traveler's perspective, it is more leisure-first than culture-first, but it is convenient, scenic, and easy to pair with a broader Hill Country trip.
destinations.safetyNotes: New Braunfels is usually straightforward for visitors in downtown, Gruene, and river recreation zones, though summer crowds can make things hectic. Stay mindful around alcohol-heavy float days, use secure parking, and keep late-night plans focused on the best-known entertainment districts.

neighborhoods.name: Gruene
neighborhoods.description: Historic entertainment district known for dance halls, shops, and river views.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown New Braunfels
neighborhoods.description: Central area with boutiques, bakeries, bars, and walkable streets.

neighborhoods.name: River Road Area
neighborhoods.description: Scenic corridor tied to tubing, rentals, and outdoor weekends.

mustVisit.name: Gruene Hall
mustVisit.description: Iconic Texas dance hall and one of the area's best-known live music stops.

mustVisit.name: Schlitterbahn
mustVisit.description: Famous waterpark that makes the city a summer magnet.

mustVisit.name: Landa Park
mustVisit.description: Large park with spring-fed water, trails, and family-friendly recreation.

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizen
destinations.ratingNotes: Texas Hill Country; predominantly white; river recreation

destinations.tagline: Vicksburg's pivotal Civil War history and its significance in African American freedom makes it a...
destinations.vibe: Vicksburg's pivotal Civil War history and its significance in African American freedom makes it an important stop on the Mississippi Freedom Trail. The National Military Park documents the siege that split the Confederacy.
destinations.safetyNotes: Vicksburg is a small Mississippi city. Downtown and the National Park are safe for visitors. Standard small-city awareness applies.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown Vicksburg
neighborhoods.description: National Military Park, historic gaming casinos on the river

neighborhoods.name: Highway 61 Blues Corridor
neighborhoods.description: Mississippi Blues Trail passes through Vicksburg

mustVisit.name: Vicksburg National Military Park
mustVisit.description: Site of the 47-day siege that split the Confederacy — 17,000 Union monuments including USCT markers

mustVisit.name: Vicksburg Battlefield Museum
mustVisit.description: Comprehensive exhibits on the battle and its significance for Black freedom

mustVisit.name: Birthplace of Coca-Cola Museum
mustVisit.description: The first bottle of Coca-Cola was bottled in Vicksburg in 1894 — quirky but fun

mustVisit.name: Old Court House Museum
mustVisit.description: Antebellum courthouse with significant Civil War and Black history exhibits

mustVisit.name: Mississippi River Levee walk
mustVisit.description: Walk the levee along the mighty Mississippi — sense of place and history

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens
destinations.ratingNotes: Civil War significance; Mississippi Black community; limited BOP

destinations.tagline: Buckroe Beach and the North End of Virginia Beach have deep Black beach culture history. The larg...
destinations.vibe: Buckroe Beach and the North End of Virginia Beach have deep Black beach culture history. The largest East Coast beach is popular with Black families from DC, Baltimore, and Richmond.
destinations.safetyNotes: Virginia Beach is generally safe and family-friendly. The resort strip and boardwalk are very accessible. Some areas of Norfolk/Hampton require standard urban awareness.

neighborhoods.name: Oceanfront/Boardwalk
neighborhoods.description: Main beach strip with 3-mile boardwalk

neighborhoods.name: Hampton Roads (nearby)
neighborhoods.description: Fort Monroe — Freedom's Fortress where enslaved people escaped to Union lines

neighborhoods.name: Norfolk (nearby)
neighborhoods.description: Norfolk State University, Slover Library, city arts scene

mustVisit.name: Virginia Beach Boardwalk
mustVisit.description: 3-mile oceanfront boardwalk with hotels, restaurants, and beach access

mustVisit.name: Hampton University (nearby)
mustVisit.description: One of America's premier HBCUs — beautiful waterfront campus in Hampton

mustVisit.name: Fort Monroe National Monument (Hampton)
mustVisit.description: Freedom's Fortress — where enslaved people escaped to freedom during Civil War

mustVisit.name: Virginia Aquarium & Marine Science Center
mustVisit.description: World-class aquarium with over 300 species

mustVisit.name: First Landing State Park
mustVisit.description: Beautiful maritime forest and beach — where English colonists first landed in 1607

destinations.bestTimeToVisit: May-September (beach season), Memorial Day weekend popular with Black travelers
destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens
destinations.ratingNotes: Buckroe Beach Black history; military town; limited BOP

destinations.tagline: Mediterranean capital energy with medina history and North African cool.
destinations.vibe: Tunis gives you markets, museums, and sea access without the same scale as Cairo or Casablanca. For Black travelers, it can feel approachable in its better-connected districts, though some visitors still report occasional staring and the need for a confident presence.
destinations.safetyNotes: Urban areas used by travelers are often manageable, but protest activity, scams, and petty theft can still affect the experience. Stay informed, use hotel-vetted transport when arriving late, and avoid political gatherings or isolated areas after dark.

neighborhoods.name: La Marsa
neighborhoods.description: Coastal suburb with cafés, beach energy, and a more relaxed feel.

neighborhoods.name: Medina of Tunis
neighborhoods.description: Historic core filled with souks, mosques, and layered architecture.

neighborhoods.name: Berges du Lac
neighborhoods.description: Modern district with hotels, business centers, and easier logistics.

mustVisit.name: Bardo National Museum
mustVisit.description: Renowned museum known for mosaics and deep historical collections.

mustVisit.name: Medina of Tunis
mustVisit.description: UNESCO-listed old city for shopping, architecture, and atmosphere.

mustVisit.name: Sidi Bou Said
mustVisit.description: Blue-and-white cliffside town just outside Tunis with iconic views.

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for U.S. citizen for short tourist stays in Tunisia; verify current entry rules before travel.
destinations.ratingNotes: Carthaginian heritage; Afro-Tunisian presence; generally welcoming

destinations.tagline: Art, adobe, and spiritual calm in the high desert
destinations.vibe: Santa Fe feels slower, more intentional, and more design-forward than most U.S. cities. For Black travelers, it is not a major diaspora hub, but it can still be powerful if you want beauty, culture, and a reflective kind of trip.
destinations.safetyNotes: Santa Fe is generally comfortable and easy for visitors, particularly around the Plaza, Canyon Road, and museum zones. The main caution is cost and transportation logistics more than personal safety, since the city spreads out once you leave the center.

neighborhoods.name: Plaza / Downtown
neighborhoods.description: Historic core with adobe architecture, museums, and classic Santa Fe atmosphere.

neighborhoods.name: Canyon Road
neighborhoods.description: Gallery-lined district that feels artistic, upscale, and distinctly Santa Fe.

neighborhoods.name: Railyard District
neighborhoods.description: More contemporary district with markets, design shops, and younger energy.

mustVisit.name: Georgia O'Keeffe Museum
mustVisit.description: Signature art stop that fits the city's aesthetic identity perfectly.

mustVisit.name: Santa Fe Plaza
mustVisit.description: Historic public square that anchors any first visit to the city.

mustVisit.name: Museum of International Folk Art
mustVisit.description: One of Santa Fe's richest museum experiences for global art and craft.

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizen
destinations.ratingNotes: High desert arts; Hispanic/Native dominant; limited BOP

destinations.tagline: Danube views, thermal baths, and nightlife with range.
destinations.vibe: Budapest gives you big architecture, nightlife, and value in one trip, which is why so many travelers leave impressed. For Black travelers, it can be exciting and easy to enjoy in the center, though the city’s politics and social climate can feel less outwardly inclusive than Western Europe.
destinations.safetyNotes: Usually manageable for visitors, but petty crime, nightlife scams, and occasional uncomfortable social interactions are worth watching for. Stick with well-trafficked central areas at night and be selective with clubs and late-night taxi situations.

neighborhoods.name: District V
neighborhoods.description: Central district with major landmarks and riverfront access.

neighborhoods.name: Jewish Quarter
neighborhoods.description: Buzzing area known for ruin bars, food, and nightlife.

neighborhoods.name: Buda Castle District
neighborhoods.description: Historic hillside area with views and calmer old-world charm.

mustVisit.name: Széchenyi Thermal Bath
mustVisit.description: One of the city’s most famous bath complexes.

mustVisit.name: Parliament Building
mustVisit.description: Signature landmark on the Danube and a Budapest icon.

mustVisit.name: Central Market Hall
mustVisit.description: Large historic market with food and souvenir stalls.

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for U.S. citizen for short tourist stays in Hungary/Schengen.
destinations.ratingNotes: Eastern Europe; documented anti-Black racism; Orban govt rhetoric

destinations.tagline: Sun, coastline, and neighborhoods that actually reward wandering
destinations.vibe: San Diego feels easy in the best way: beaches, tacos, museums, and distinct neighborhoods without the same pressure as LA. For a Black traveler, it is strongest when you mix the coast with Balboa Park, Southeast history, and a few neighborhood-based food stops instead of staying inside a resort bubble the whole time.
destinations.safetyNotes: San Diego is one of the smoother big-city visitor experiences in the batch, especially in coastal neighborhoods, Balboa Park, and the downtown core. Usual city precautions still apply around nightlife and parking, but the overall pace is more manageable than many comparable destinations.

neighborhoods.name: Gaslamp Quarter
neighborhoods.description: Downtown nightlife district with restaurants, bars, and event energy.

neighborhoods.name: North Park
neighborhoods.description: Favorite for coffee, craft food, bars, and a more local creative crowd.

neighborhoods.name: La Jolla
neighborhoods.description: Coastal enclave with views, beach access, and polished dining.

mustVisit.name: Balboa Park
mustVisit.description: Cultural mega-hub with gardens, museums, and iconic San Diego scenery.

mustVisit.name: Museum of Us
mustVisit.description: Balboa Park museum with anthropology-focused exhibits and a strong setting.

mustVisit.name: La Jolla Cove
mustVisit.description: Classic coastal stop for views, seals, and that San Diego glow.

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizen
destinations.ratingNotes: Military city; Logan Heights Black history; moderate community

destinations.tagline: Chocolate City — Black power, Black excellence
destinations.vibe: Chocolate City — where Black political power, intellectual culture, and Howard University pride converge.
destinations.safetyNotes: DC is generally very safe for tourists, especially around the National Mall, Capitol Hill, and U Street. The U Street Corridor and Shaw neighborhood are historically Black areas now buzzing with restaurants and nightlife. Metro is excellent and reliable.

neighborhoods.name: U Street Corridor
neighborhoods.description: Once called the 'Black Broadway' — Duke Ellington's neighborhood. Now filled with Black-owned restaurants, bars, and cultural spaces.

neighborhoods.name: Shaw
neighborhoods.description: Historic Black neighborhood adjacent to U Street with Howard University energy, great dining, and thriving arts scene.

neighborhoods.name: Capitol Hill
neighborhoods.description: Historic row houses, Eastern Market, and the seat of American power. Beautiful to walk and explore.

neighborhoods.name: Adams Morgan
neighborhoods.description: International food scene, eclectic bars, and a diverse neighborhood with great energy on 18th Street.

mustVisit.name: National Museum of African American History & Culture
mustVisit.description: The crown jewel of the Smithsonian. Plan 4-6 hours minimum. Tickets are timed and in high demand — book weeks ahead.

mustVisit.name: Howard University Campus
mustVisit.description: The Mecca. Walking the yard at HU is a powerful experience — the legacy here is palpable.

mustVisit.name: Ben's Chili Bowl
mustVisit.description: DC institution since 1958. Half-smokes and chili on U Street. The Obamas eat here. You should too.

mustVisit.name: Martin Luther King Jr. Memorial
mustVisit.description: A moving tribute on the National Mall. The inscription on the Stone of Hope is unforgettable.

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens
destinations.ratingNotes: Chocolate City; Black political capital; strong BOP

destinations.tagline: Wilmington is the site of the 1898 Wilmington Massacre — the only successful coup d'état in US hi...
destinations.vibe: Wilmington is the site of the 1898 Wilmington Massacre — the only successful coup d'état in US history when white supremacists overthrew the Black and Biracial government. The history museum here is essential and the beaches (Wrightsville, Carolina Beach) are excellent.
destinations.safetyNotes: Wilmington is generally safe for tourists. The historic district and beach communities are accessible. The city has been growing rapidly with a diverse newcomer population.

neighborhoods.name: Historic Downtown
neighborhoods.description: Wilmington's vibrant riverfront with 1898 Massacre history nearby

neighborhoods.name: Wrightsville Beach
neighborhoods.description: NC's most popular beach — 5 miles of beautiful Atlantic Coast beach

neighborhoods.name: Carolina Beach / Kure Beach
neighborhoods.description: More affordable beach towns south of Wrightsville — Fort Fisher historic site

mustVisit.name: 1898 Wilmington Race Riot Memorial
mustVisit.description: Memorial to the 1898 coup where white supremacists destroyed Wilmington's Black governance — essential history

mustVisit.name: Cape Fear Museum
mustVisit.description: Documents 1898 massacre and broader Wilmington Black history

mustVisit.name: Wrightsville Beach
mustVisit.description: Beautiful NC coast beach with excellent water clarity and beach towns

mustVisit.name: Bellamy Mansion Museum
mustVisit.description: Antebellum mansion with exhibits on enslaved labor that built it

mustVisit.name: Screen Gems Studios Tour
mustVisit.description: Major film studio — many Black films shot here including 'Dawson's Creek'

destinations.bestTimeToVisit: April-October (beach season), year-round for history visits
destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens
destinations.ratingNotes: 1898 massacre history; Wilmington reckoning; complex legacy

destinations.tagline: America's first national park — Old Faithful, prismatic springs, bison herds, and geothermal wond...
destinations.vibe: America's first national park — Old Faithful, prismatic springs, bison herds, and geothermal wonders. The Black outdoors movement has been vocal about reclaiming public lands, with Yellowstone as a major symbol.
destinations.safetyNotes: Yellowstone is safe but requires wildlife awareness — do not approach bison or bears. Thermal areas are extremely dangerous. Gateway towns (Jackson, Cody, Gardiner) are generally welcoming.

neighborhoods.name: Old Faithful District
neighborhoods.description: The most visited area of the park — predictable geyser every 60-110 minutes

neighborhoods.name: Mammoth Hot Springs
neighborhoods.description: Park headquarters with travertine terraces — army's first presence here

neighborhoods.name: Jackson Hole (gateway)
neighborhoods.description: World-class resort town — expensive but stunning

mustVisit.name: Old Faithful Geyser
mustVisit.description: The world's most famous geyser — erupts approximately every 90 minutes

mustVisit.name: Grand Prismatic Spring
mustVisit.description: America's largest hot spring — rainbow colors visible from a distance, stunning from overlook trail

mustVisit.name: Lamar Valley Wildlife Watch
mustVisit.description: America's Serengeti — bison, wolves, bears, and elk congregate here

mustVisit.name: Grand Canyon of the Yellowstone
mustVisit.description: Yellow canyon walls and spectacular waterfalls — Upper and Lower Falls

mustVisit.name: Mammoth Hot Springs Terraces
mustVisit.description: Alien landscape of colored travertine thermal terraces

destinations.bestTimeToVisit: June-September (winter closures affect most roads and visitor areas)
destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens
destinations.ratingNotes: America's first national park; predominantly white; very limited BOP

destinations.tagline: Willamette Valley ease with gardens, history, and a softer pace
destinations.vibe: Salem is not flashy, but it can be a pleasant Oregon stop if you want wineries, gardens, and a smaller capital-city feel. For a Black traveler, it reads more as a calm regional base than a culture-first destination, with the best value coming from nearby valley day trips and a low-stress downtown.
destinations.safetyNotes: Salem is generally manageable for visitors, especially downtown, riverfront, and vineyard-oriented outings in the daytime. As with many smaller cities, things can get quiet at night, so it is best approached as a planned, drive-based destination rather than a nightlife city.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown Salem
neighborhoods.description: Core area with shops, restaurants, museums, and the capitol nearby.

neighborhoods.name: Riverfront Area
neighborhoods.description: Park-focused zone with open space and family-friendly events.

neighborhoods.name: South Central / Bush Park Area
neighborhoods.description: Historic residential pocket near gardens and museums.

mustVisit.name: Oregon State Capitol
mustVisit.description: Defining landmark of the city and an easy anchor for first-time visitors.

mustVisit.name: Willamette Heritage Center
mustVisit.description: Museum complex that gives Salem more historical texture.

mustVisit.name: Riverfront City Park
mustVisit.description: Simple, pleasant outdoor stop that works well with a downtown day.

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizen

destinations.tagline: Laid-back twin-island escape with beaches, reefs, and roots
destinations.vibe: Tobago feels softer and slower than Trinidad, with a beach-and-village rhythm that rewards people who do not need a packed itinerary. For Black travelers, the island’s Afro-Caribbean presence makes the vibe feel culturally familiar in a good way.
destinations.safetyNotes: Generally easier and calmer than many larger islands, though secluded beaches and late-night roads call for common-sense caution. Rentals are useful, and it is smart not to leave valuables visible when beach-hopping.

neighborhoods.name: Crown Point
neighborhoods.description: Main visitor zone near the airport, beaches, and nightlife.

neighborhoods.name: Scarborough
neighborhoods.description: Island capital with markets, ferry links, and local city energy.

neighborhoods.name: Speyside
neighborhoods.description: Northeast village popular for diving and quieter nature stays.

mustVisit.name: Pigeon Point Heritage Park
mustVisit.description: Iconic Tobago beach area with calm water and classic views.

mustVisit.name: Nylon Pool / Buccoo Reef
mustVisit.description: Signature boat excursion for reef scenery and shallow turquoise water.

mustVisit.name: Argyle Falls
mustVisit.description: Popular inland waterfall for a break from the beach circuit.

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for U.S. citizen for short tourist stays
destinations.ratingNotes: Laid-back twin island; Black-majority; reef and roots

destinations.tagline: A college-town crossroads with more history than hype
destinations.vibe: Terre Haute is a low-key Midwest stop that works best if you like under-the-radar cities with local character. From a Black travel perspective, it's more of a thoughtful weekend than a scene-heavy trip, with museums, historic districts, and a steady college-town rhythm.
destinations.safetyNotes: Terre Haute is generally manageable for visitors, especially around campus zones, downtown, and major attractions in daytime. Nightlife is limited, so it is best approached as a drive-and-park city rather than a late-night wandering destination.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown Terre Haute
neighborhoods.description: Historic center with theaters, museums, and civic landmarks.

neighborhoods.name: 12 Points District
neighborhoods.description: Reviving district known for murals, local businesses, and community events.

neighborhoods.name: Farrington Grove
neighborhoods.description: Historic district with older homes and a residential college-town feel.

mustVisit.name: CANDLES Holocaust Museum
mustVisit.description: Powerful educational museum and one of the city's best-known cultural stops.

mustVisit.name: Swope Art Museum
mustVisit.description: Compact art museum that gives the downtown core more cultural depth.

mustVisit.name: Indiana Theatre
mustVisit.description: Restored historic theater that anchors downtown's old-school charm.

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizen

destinations.tagline: The Spice Island — paradise with deep African roots
destinations.vibe: The Spice Island — white sand, turquoise water, Swahili culture, and deep African history.
destinations.safetyNotes: Zanzibar is extremely safe for tourists. Stone Town, the beaches of Nungwi, Kendwa, and Paje are all very welcoming. Respect local Muslim culture — dress modestly when outside beach areas. The island is peaceful and the Swahili people are famously warm and hospitable.

neighborhoods.name: Stone Town
neighborhoods.description: UNESCO-listed old city — winding alleys, carved wooden doors, Arab-African architecture, and spice markets.

neighborhoods.name: Nungwi
neighborhoods.description: North tip of the island — stunning white sand, clear turquoise water, dhow sunset cruises, and beach bars.

neighborhoods.name: Paje
neighborhoods.description: East coast beach village — kitesurfing capital of East Africa, relaxed vibes, and incredible seafood.

neighborhoods.name: Kendwa
neighborhoods.description: Pristine beach near Nungwi known for full moon parties and spectacular sunsets.

mustVisit.name: Stone Town Walking Tour
mustVisit.description: Navigate the UNESCO labyrinth of Stone Town with a local guide — Arab-African architecture, slave market history, and history at every corner.

mustVisit.name: Spice Farm Tour
mustVisit.description: Zanzibar earned its Spice Island name — taste and smell cloves, nutmeg, vanilla, and cinnamon growing on a local farm. Unforgettable.

mustVisit.name: Dhow Sunset Cruise
mustVisit.description: Sail on a traditional Swahili dhow at sunset with fresh seafood and cold drinks. One of those perfect travel moments.

mustVisit.name: Darajani Market
mustVisit.description: Stone Town's bustling fresh market — fish, spices, fruits, and local street food. Go early for the best experience.

destinations.visaInfo: e-Visa required. Apply at eservices.immigration.go.tz. US citizens eligible.
destinations.ratingNotes: Stone Town welcoming; predominantly Black-Muslim culture; low risk

destinations.tagline: Four-season outdoors with a downtown that feels usable
destinations.vibe: Spokane mixes river scenery, a practical downtown, and enough arts and food to keep a long weekend interesting. For a Black traveler, it is more about ease, nature access, and local rhythm than diaspora depth, but it can still be a solid Pacific Northwest city break if you want something less obvious.
destinations.safetyNotes: Spokane is fairly approachable for visitors in downtown, South Hill, and riverfront areas, though you should still use city awareness after dark. Stick to active districts, secure your car, and treat it as a destination where daytime exploring usually shines more than late-night wandering.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown Spokane
neighborhoods.description: Main core with hotels, restaurants, museums, and Riverfront Park access.

neighborhoods.name: Kendall Yards
neighborhoods.description: Newer riverfront-adjacent district with views, dining, and polished energy.

neighborhoods.name: South Hill / Perry District
neighborhoods.description: Leafier neighborhood area with coffee shops, boutiques, and a slower pace.

mustVisit.name: Riverfront Park
mustVisit.description: Signature downtown park with river views and year-round city life.

mustVisit.name: Northwest Museum of Arts and Culture
mustVisit.description: Best museum stop for regional history and arts.

mustVisit.name: Kendall Yards
mustVisit.description: One of the most visitor-friendly areas for food, views, and a clean base.

destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizen
destinations.ratingNotes: Eastern Washington; very limited Black community

destinations.tagline: Zion has become a top destination for Black outdoor enthusiasts embracing public lands. The Narro...
destinations.vibe: Zion has become a top destination for Black outdoor enthusiasts embracing public lands. The Narrows, Angels Landing, and stunning canyon walls make Zion one of America's most spectacular parks.
destinations.safetyNotes: Zion is safe but physically demanding. Angels Landing has a fatal fall history — chain sections are used. The Narrows flash flood risk requires weather monitoring. Springdale (gateway town) is very welcoming.

neighborhoods.name: Springdale (gateway)
neighborhoods.description: Gateway to Zion — hotels, restaurants, and shuttle to the park

neighborhoods.name: St. George (nearby city)
neighborhoods.description: Nearest substantial city with more accommodation options

mustVisit.name: The Narrows
mustVisit.description: Hike through the Virgin River canyon — wading between walls 2,000 feet tall

mustVisit.name: Angels Landing
mustVisit.description: Thrilling permitted hike to a peak with sheer 1,488-foot drops — requires advance permit

mustVisit.name: Canyon Overlook Trail
mustVisit.description: Easy 1-mile trail with stunning canyon views — great for families

mustVisit.name: Emerald Pools Trails
mustVisit.description: Multiple pool levels with hanging gardens and waterfalls

mustVisit.name: Zion Canyon Scenic Drive (shuttle)
mustVisit.description: Free shuttle through the canyon — stop at any trailhead

destinations.bestTimeToVisit: March-May, September-October (avoid summer heat and crowds, winter trail closures)
destinations.visaInfo: No visa required for US citizens
destinations.ratingNotes: Utah national park; predominantly white; limited Black infrastructure
